methods.name: Stage-Progression

data.narrative:
Vatn Systems is a Series A-stage defense and security company founded by Nelson Mills, currently valued at $300M post-money following $76.5M in cumulative fundraising. The valuation analysis draws from a comparable universe of 10 companies across the Defense & Security sector—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms including Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Castelion, and Defense Unicorns.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis applies a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A advancement, yielding $600M. Peer-median analysis across 9 same-sector and same/adjacent-stage comparables produces a $1.0B implied value, derived from a median peer valuation of $1.0B against a range of $60M to $1.5B. The sample includes Series B peers valued between $1.0B and $1.5B (Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI), grounding the benchmark in observable market data.

Triangulating these methods produces a valuation range of $510M (low) to $1.1B (high), with a median of $800M. The current $300M post-money valuation sits materially below this range, suggesting either discount to observable peer comparables or material execution risk not yet reflected in the peer set. Key uncertainties include how Vatn's stage positioning maps to the median peer cohort (predominantly Series B) and whether the peer range adequately captures sector-specific defensibility or revenue scale differentials.
